blockingIssues.description: Commit 9730db9 only adds a duplicate recovered handoff-key test and renderer key fix, but it does not add the broader Mission verification coverage promised for interaction-mode hot switching, session persistence, notification mapping, watcher/recovery scenarios, or the VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-007 end-to-end flows.

Mission E2E preflight cleanup was performed manually during verification, but the repository still leaves `dev:test` as `ELECTRON_REMOTE_DEBUGGING_PORT=9222 electron-vite dev`, so stale isolated Electron listeners on 9222, 5173, and 3002 are not automatically cleared by the product or test harness.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in `mission-verification-worker` that for narrowly scoped UI-warning or read-only validation fixes, workers may reuse an existing isolated `Mission-GUI-TEST` Mission session instead of creating a fresh Mission-invoking flow, as long as they stay on the Electron surface and explicitly record the reuse in verification evidence.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The review for `mission-integration-tests-and-e2e-readiness` found the worker's only procedural deviation was reusing an existing isolated Mission session to verify duplicate React-key warning removal. The handoff explicitly marked `followedProcedure: false` and explained that creating a fresh Mission run would have been unnecessarily destructive for this read-only UI validation.
